name: Cruzcampo Sevilla Orange Flavoured Beer 24x440ml Cans - NEW
description: 3.3% ABV Spanish Lager Pack size: 24 X 440ML Cruzcampo Sevilla Orange, a crisp and refreshing lager brewed with the distinct zesty flavour of Seville Oranges. Cruzcampo Sevilla Orange delivers a perfectly balanced beer. Blending Cruzcampo’s refreshing pilsner profile with subtle notes of Seville orange flavour, the result is a hoppy aroma with zesty citrus notes, creating a lager that’s light, crisp and beautifully sessionable.

name: Crystal Head Vodka 3 litre
description: Ghostbuster’s actor and comedian Dan Aykroyd together with John Alexander have produced a pure, smooth vodka with one of the cleanest palates out there. This isn't just a prop, it's serious Vodka. Crystal Head was awarded a double gold medal at the San Francisco World Spirits Competition. This award-winning 3 litre bottle of Canadian Vodka starts out with grain from the fields of Ontario and water from a glacial aquifer in Newfoundland. Although Crystal Head is quadruple-distilled, they decided to filter it through 500-million-year-old crystals, called Herkimer diamonds, brought down to bottling strength using Newfoundland water. It is the housed within a massive crystal skull made by Bruni Glass in Italy. The Crystal Head Magnum skull is made by Milan-based manufacturer Bruni Glass, it is seriously impressive. We couldn’t imagine our drinks cabinet without the characterful skull bottle of his impossibly pure Crystal Head Vodka Comes in the manufacturer's presentation box.

name: Crystal Head Vodka 70cl
description: Alc Vol: 40% Size: 70cl Having recently won a double gold at the prestigious San Francisco Spirits awards, proving the quality of the liquid to be equal to the bottle design, Crystal Head Vodka has one of the cleanest palates out there and little to no burn at the finish. One of our most popular vodkas Crystal Head has a taste to match the bottle design. Double winner of the San Francisco world spirit competition. Dan Aykroyds crystal head Vodka is the purest form of vodka going through a quadruple distillation process and then filtered through 500 year old crystals making the vodka free of any additives.

name: Cuckoo Supernova Gin Miniature - 5cl
description: ABV: 44% SIZE: 5cl The Cuckoo range continues to be one of our most popular gin collections, created just next door in Brindle Distillery, Lancashire. Their base of Holmes Farm provides the secret to their success - fresh spring water from their artesian aquifer, many botanicals grown on the farm or locally sourced, and even a natural source of honey from their beehives, sustained to help the bee community thrive. Their lively Supernova Gin explodes with fresh grapefruit and luscious pomelo. A 100% real citrus fruit gin with no added sugar, it shows a natural blush colour at a punchy 44% abv. Crisp grapefruit alongside lovely notes of juniper and subtle sweetness from tropical Pomelo. Serve with a premium Indian tonic, lots of ice, and a slice of grapefruit. Brindle Distillery was the idea of Gerard and Cath Singleton to diversify Holmes Farm, which has been in the family since 1930. The meticulous quality of their gin and spirits production has meant the idea surpassing expectations, with the national reputation of the Cuckoo range leading to a plethora of awards and the establishment of their very own bar and events space on-site, The Cuckoo's Nest.

name: Cuervo Tequila Plastic Shot Glasses - Pack of 10
description: pack of 10 reusable plastic shot glasses strong & durable

name: Cullinan View Chenin Blanc Rosé 75cl
description: Vibrant rosé made in an off-dry style, lovely crisp acidity with summer berry fruit flavours. Region- Western Cape Primary Grape - Chenin Blanc Taste Expression - Dry, elegant rosés

name: Cuperly NV Rose Champagne 75cl
description: Salmon pink easy drinking Champagne. Delicate in style with tones of summer fruits. Delicious! Created in 1845 production of Cuperly sits at 300,000 bottles per year which is mainly distributed in France and is still family owned. Champagne Cuperly uses only grapes selected from the Grande Crus of Montagne de Reims (40% Chardonnay/60% Pinot Noir) this gives the Champagne fantastic freshness from the Chardonnay plus a richness and complexity from the Pinot Noir, to maintain freshness malolactic fermentation is avoided. This means Champagne Cuperly is bang on trend with UK wine drinkers taste profile.

name: Cut to the Smoke Real Rum 70cl
description: Alc Vol: 40% Size: 70cl The first smoked Rum in the UK. Infused with Arabica Coffee Beans, Smoked with Burnt Wood Chips and left to rest in Oak Barrels. THIS RUM IS ONE OF A KIND ON THE NOSE COFFEE █████ COCOA NIB ████ █████ OAKED VANILLA ███ PIPE TOBACCO ████████ ON THE TASTE █████ BROWN SUGAR ██████ LEATHER ██████ CHARRED WOOD ███████ MATCHSTICK SMOKE

name: Cuvee Rousson Dry White Wine 75cl
description: A very drinkable French white wine with apple and lemon notes on a smooth, gentle palate.

name: Cuvee Rousson Medium Dry White 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 10% Size: 75cl Country: France Off-dry in style, with ripe fruit aromas and a honeysuckle twist.

name: Cuvee Rousson Syrah Red 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 13% Size: 75cl Country: France Grape Variety: Shiraz Region: Vin de Pays d’Oc A real taste of the South of France, soft and smooth with gentle red and black berry fruit flavours.

name: Cuvee St Jaine Red Wine 75cl
description: ABV - 12.5% Size 75cl Country: France A Full round red table wine. Fresh ripe fruit, grown in the south of france, makes this blend of Carignan, Grenache and Cabernet Sauvignon a delight to the palate. With its silky and long lasting finish it is the perfect accompaniment for quality meats. Allergens - Contains: Sulphites *Some labels may be scuffed

name: D’usse VSOP Cognac 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 40% Size: 75cl D'USSÉ is the legendary Cognac brand founded by Jay-Z. Jay-z brings out his finest masterpiece, Dusse VSOP Cognac is all things great. Now available at 365drinks.co.uk. With a palate-arousing bouquet of rich woody notes, spicy cinnamon, creamy almond, layers of floral notes and sweet honey accents, D'USSE VSOP Cognac is a bold, distinctive and truly modern expression of this classic liquor. A truly modern expression of cognac, D'USSE VSOP is a cool, confident and charismatic expression of this long-adored liquor, elevating a classic to contemporary highs and often seen socialising on the celeb circuit. Naturally aged for a minimum of four and a half years, this bold and full-bodied blend is made from a selection of the finest eaux-de-vie that were carefully matured in the cellars of the Chaueau de Cognac itself. The result is a palate-arousing bouquet that’s bold and rich in woody notes, with layers of spicy cinnamon and creamy almond, with floral highs and subtle accents of honey and dried fruit. An enigmatic charmer of a cognac, D’USSÉ VSOP Cognac is one smooth customer. Perfect Serve: Fix up a D’USSÉ Champagne Sparkler. One part D’USSÉ VSOP Cognac, one sugar cube, a couple of dashes of bitters, topped up with sparkling wine. Serve in a flute and garnish with an orange twist. The perfect tipple for toasting everyday life.

name: Dacastello Chianti DOCG Red Wine 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 13% Size: 75cl A World famous Wine, produced in a unique and exceptional area that taken as a whole is the biggest D.O.C.G district in Italy. Grapes from the centre of Tuscany between Florence and Siena are used to make this bright, ruby-red wine with its distinctive herbaceous and red fruit aromas. The blend is 70% Sangiovese and 30% Canaiolo Nero, a black-skinned Tuscan grape that was the main constituent of Chianti up until the late 19th century.

name: Dacastello Merlot Veneto I.G.T. 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 12% Size; 75cl GRAPE: 100% Merlot Merlot is thought to be named after the merlet (the French name for blackbird), These birds being known to have a particular fondness for its tasty berries. And if the blackbird is an animal associated with curiosity, Merlot is a wine associated with contact. International and straightforward, Merlot Veneto I.G.T. Dacastello celebrates meetings, in Italy and around the world. COLOUR Ruby red BOUQUET Pleasant and delicate, with fruity notes of cherry and red berries PALATE Well-structured and harmonious, with a persistent aftertaste PAIRING Perfect with pasta, barbecued meat, noodles, kofta and pakora.

name: Dacastello Pinot Grigio - Delle Venezie DOC 75cl
description: ABV - 12% vol. Size: 75cl This beautifully subtle white wine has a bright straw yellow colour and is made from pinot grigio grapes grown in the wonderful vineyards of Italy's Veneto region. Its wild flower and acacia fragrance leads on to subtle flavours of the same and on the palate you will experence an harmonius, dry wine with a persistent finish. COLOUR: Straw yellow BOUQUET: Herbaceous and fragrant PALATE: Dry, balanced and harmonious PAIRING: I deal with fish dishes, white meats and eggs. It is the perfect accompaniment for international cuisine

name: Dacastello Prosecco D.O.C. 75cl
description: Size - 75cl ABV - 11% A favourite wine of the wife of Emperor Augustus in ancient Rome, Prosecco was also the nectar of the gods served at receptions at the Hapsburg court. Prosecco D.O.C. Treviso Millesimato Extra Dry Dacastello, an echo of la dolce vita.

name: Dalwhinnie 15 Year Old Single Malt Scotch Whisky
description: ABV: 43% Size: 70cl Dalwhinnie 15 Year Old is a highland Single Malt from the highest distillery and coldest in Scotland. Winner of San Francisco World Spirits Competition Gold 2016. A smooth, aromatic flavour with heather-honey sweetness. The clean, heather flavours call for a neat serve, and perhaps a little still spring water. From Scotland's highest and coldest distillery it is aged for 15 years to develop a smooth heather-honeyed charm. Made in the highest and coldest working distillery in Scotland, with water from a loch at 2000 feet, Dalwhinnie whisky thrives in extreme conditions - creating a liquid as sweet and accessible as its highland home is remote. Smooth and aromatic, Dalwhinnie 15 Year Old is clean and fresh, with pears, a touch of toffee and a soft floral note. Its palate is fruity and rich with notes of steamed syrup pudding and sponge cake and even a little cinnamon and nutmeg. Each sip finishes with just enough spice to match the fruit creating a lovely balance. This award winning Malt received San Francisco World Spirits Competition Gold 2016 and to appreciate it fully should be enjoyed neat, or with a drop of still spring water. Dalwhinnie 15 Year Old makes an ideal gift as an introduction to Dalwhinnie and Single Malt Whiskies

name: Dalwhinnie Winter's Gold Single Malt Scotch Whisky 70cl
description: ABV: 43% Size: 70cl Dalwhinnie Winter's Gold is a 2015 addition to the Highland distillery's range. It's inspired by the chilly climate up at Scotland's highest and coldest distillery. As its label states it was ‘crafted by the cold'. Dalwhinnie Winter's Gold is an innovative bottling from Diageo offering a different kind of whisky drinking experience. Dalwhinnie Winter's Gold is in the same vein as the 15 Year Old with aromas of flowers, honeyed with a hint of peat and green fruit - apples and fresh pear. Each sip is soft on the palate, with slightly warming honeyed fruit. The finish offers a soothing texture, as light floral notes mingle with soft fruits and just a touch of spice. Made using whisky distilled in the cold Scottish winter, it is intended to be served straight from the freezer, delivering richness and sweetness as the glass warms up. Winter's Gold is the perfect gift for drinkers looking for an introduction to Dalwhinnie and single malt whiskies

name: Darnley’s Small Batch, Navy Strength, London Dry Gin 70cl - 57.1% ABV
description: ABV: 57.1% Size: 70cl This almighty 57.1% ABV, award winning, Navy Strength gin edition of Darnley’s Spiced Gin coming in at 57.1% ABV was inspired by the illustrious Royal Navy career of the family ancestor Sir Rosslyn Wemyss who commanded ships around the world and rose to the rank of Admiral of the Fleet. He was the senior British representative at the signing of the Armistice and it was he who made the decision to have the ceasefire come into effect at 11.00a.m effectively saving hundreds of lives. His ships officers enjoyed a daily ration of gin that had to pass a gunpowder test to prove it was over 57% alcohol and therefore Navy Strength. This gin uses the same warming spice botanicals of our Spiced Gin but with more juniper. Great served with quality tonic, a wedge of pink grapefruit and lots of ice! Award-Winning Scottish London Dry Gin

name: De Kuyper Blue Curacao 50cl
description: ABV 20% Size: 50cl For cocktails Somehow the colour blue always reminds us of a tropical island in the sun. The essence of this liqueur is the Curacao or Lahara fruit. Capturing the soul of this fabulous fruit makes a distinctive and unique liqueur. Famous for the Blue Lagoon cocktail and making drinks look, well, blue.

name: De Kuyper Caramel Syrup
description: Alc Vol: 0.0% Size; 70cl De Kuyper Caramel Syrup is based on aromatic caramel from Belgium that is full of flavours. This syrup reminds you of sweet, buttery caramel and adds a beautiful, warm colour to comfort dessert drinks.

name: De Kuyper Cherry Brandy 50cl
description: ABV 24% Size 50cl De Kuyper's Cherry Brandy is lightly spiced and bursting with red cherry sweetness. Cocktails benefit from its fruity, lightly nutty hit of flavour. Extracts from dark red cherries, subtly enhanced with exotic spices, blended with fine brandy. Incomparable almond flavour derived from crushing the cherry kernels for that smooth, fruity and warming flavour.

name: De Kuyper Crème de Menthe Liqueur 50cl
description: Alc Vol: 24% Bott: 50cl It's not easy being green' a famous entertainment frog once stated. Surely, the frog had never tasted our vibrant and fresh Crème de Menthe Green yet. The flashy green liqueur showcases the essential oils of both crisp mint leaves and lemon peels, beautifully blended in this liqueur with just the right amount of sugar and alcohol. You can't go wrong shaking this in a kickass Grasshopper cocktail! Dazzling taste of crisp mint for ultimate freshness and sweetness For cocktails

name: De Kuyper Triple Sec 50cl
description: ABV 20% Size: 50cl For cocktails, Light-style curaçao triple sec for frisky margaritas In his ever-going quest to innovate his liqueurs, our beloved master distiller found himself cruising the Caribbean in search for the ultimate oranges to craft his Curaçao Triple Sec. Back home in the Dutch city of Schiedam the master distiller left friends and family and locked himself in the distillery with only one mission. After years and years, the Master Distiller freed himself and walked out of the distillery with ultimate Curaçao Triple Sec at 20% alcohol. Keep those refreshing Margaritas coming please!

name: Dead Man’s Fingers Banana Rum 1.75 Litre
description: Size - 1.75L ABV - 37.5% Down in Cornwall, the Dead Man's Fingers folks have been making some marvellous flavoured variations of their rum, including this banana flavoured edition right here! Ought to do well in cocktails that could use a good helping of tropical vibrancy. Tasting Notes: Banana fritters, black pepper, cinnamon and honeycomb. San Francisco World Spirits Competition Silver Winner 2022

name: Dead Man's Fingers Banana Rum 5cl Miniature
description: ABV - 37.5% Size: 5cl - Miniature Down in Cornwall, the Dead Man's Fingers folks have been making some marvellous flavoured variations of their rum, including this banana flavoured edition right here! Ought to do well in cocktails that could use a good helping of tropical vibrancy. Tasting Notes: Banana fritters, black pepper, cinnamon and honeycomb.

name: Dead Man's Fingers Blue Raspberry Tequila Cream 70cl
description: Alc Vol: 40% Size: 70cl This smooth, velvety cream liqueur with aromas of fresh raspberries is as bold in flavour as in colour. Drink it neat, chilled or whip it up into a hardshake.

name: Dead Man's Fingers Cherry Rum 5cl Miniature
description: Alcohol Vol.: 37.5% Size: 5cl Miniature If you love the sweet flavour of Cherry, then your taste buds will thank you with this variation of Dead Man’s Fingers. Inspired by our original Spiced Rum from the Rum & Crab Shack in St. Ives, Cornwall, England, this rum has tasting notes of cardamom, cinnamon, brown sugar, pineapple, with clear cherry influence running through it. Signature Serve: Dead Man’s Fingers Cherry Cola! Enjoy 50ml Dead Man's Fingers Cherry Rum, 200ml Cola and a wedge of lime International Spirits Challenge Gold Winner 2022

name: Dead Man's Fingers Cherry Rum 70cl
description: Alc Vol: 37.5% Size: 70cl If you love the sweet flavour of Cherry, then your taste buds will thank you with this variation of Dead Man's Fingers. Inspired by our original Spiced Rum from the Rum & Crab Shack in St. Ives, Cornwall, England, this rum has tasting notes of cardamom, cinnamon, brown sugar, pineapple, with clear cherry influence running through it Inside the Rum and Crab Shack, a famous seafood joint in St Ives, Cornwall; and feeling suitably liberated from the tyranny of convention, a spiced rum was born; Dead Man's Fingers. Dead Man's Fingers rejected crusty old tradition in favour of flavours new; unusual flavours like Cornish Saffron cake and Pedro Ximénez ice cream were encouraged to get all close and personal with nutmeg, vanilla and a hint of orange. The result was a unique Spiced Rum that's not only delicious sipped neat but also plays well with others - that subtle orange back-note making it a better mixer for a more diverse range of cocktails. Serving suggestion: This rum has aromas of rich cherry, with notes of vanilla and zesty orange. Great in a Cherry Bellini or for something simple and refreshing. Add cherry cola or cranberry juice, ice and a fresh cherry garnish San Francisco World Spirits Competition Double Gold Winner 2021

description: Alc Vol: 37.5% Size: 70cl The taste of raspberry ripples through this rum. Inspired by our original Spiced Rum from the Rum & Crab Shack in St. Ives, Cornwall, England. Dead Man's Fingers Raspberry Rum features notes of juicy sweet ripe raspberries with a hint of citrus. This rum is designed to be versatile. It will make a superb raspberry Daiquiri, or for something simple and refreshing, add lemonade, ice, and a wedge of lime or handful of raspberries. Taste juicy sweet ripe raspberries and citrus, Inspired by Dead Man's Fingers original Spiced Rum

name: Dead Man's Fingers Raspberry Rum Cream Liqueur 70cl
description: Alc Vol: 17% Size: 70cl This is the most luxurious take on Dead Man’s Fingers yet. So, it’s a bony thumbs up to its creators… About a decade ago, scientists found out that the centre of the galaxy was filled with ethyl formate, the substance that gives raspberries their flavour and rum its smell. So, if you were able to experience the heart of the Milky Way, it would be something like raspberries and rum. Combine that with smooth and velvety cream and you have an out-of-this-world experience on your hands. This super-innovative Raspberry Rum Cream Liqueur is a collaboration between Cornwall’s Dead Man’s Fingers and flavourphile Will Borrell, the creator of the much-loved Vestal Vodka. This dream team have worked their fingers to the bone to produce a series of punchy flavoured rums to die for. The flavour here is an enhancement to what was already an exceptional rum. The base is an award-winning, two-year-old Caribbean rum, full of toasty notes, baked cake, roasted nuts, vanilla and clove-y spices. To that, they’ve added tangy raspberry and luxurious cream. Sip it neat over ice, drizzle it onto ice cream, mix it into a Raspberry White Russian or use it to create a decadent Raspberry Espresso Martini (50ml of Raspberry Rum Cream Liqueur, 20ml of Frangelico, 50ml of cold brew coffee, shake and strain). To be honest, it’s so good, we’re tempted to bathe in it. We had imagined that a flagon of this rum was prized from the gripping skeletal claws of a drowned Cornish pirate lost to Davy Jones’ Locker. And that the owners of The Rum & Crab Shack in St Ives had painstakingly recreated the elixir between shifts of boiling and broiling seafaring fare. However, it may not have gone down quite that way. The name ‘Dead Man’s Fingers’ is also what those in-the-know call the feathery gills that are removed when dressing a crab. (Now we’re hungry as well as thirsty.) But whether this exceptional rum gets its name from dark magic and sinister skulduggery or simply a macabre culinary term, Dead Man’s Fingers deserves its cult following.

description: Dead Man Fingers Rum & Glass Gift Set 2 x 5cl Spiced Rum Where a blended rum meets exotic spices to create a unique and distinctive flavour profile. Next comes a whisper of creamy caramel followed by vanilla, cinnamon, nutmeg and of course, those subtly sweet undertones of orange. Some have even found notes of pineapple, dried raisins and a pinch of black pepper - but we think they're just showing off. Mango Rum Exotic, tropical, and delicious. It's what happens when sweet, sweet mangoes and our original Spiced Rum get all close and personal. You'll experience a whiff of vanilla, the tang of citrus, and, of course, juicy-juicy mango. Serve chilled over ice with lemonade and a wedge of lime, and pow; summer just got sexy.

name: Death's Door Gin 70cl
description: Alc Vol: 47% Size: 70cl Using juniper berries that grow wild on Washington Island with coriander and fennel sourced from within the state, Death's Door Spirits is able to showcase how complementary and complex simple expressions can be. Like our vodka, Death's Door Gin employs a base of Washington Island wheat and malted barley from Chilton, Wisconsin. Death's Door Gin has a full London Dry flavour without all of the bitterness because of our extraction process and the base spirit we use (i.e., our vodka). In fact, you can taste all three botanicals: loaded juniper berries up front; spicy, citrusy notes from the coriander seeds in the mid-palate; and a soft, cooling finish provided by the fennel seeds. Death's Door Gin is big enough to stand up to classic or vintage cocktail treatment, but soft enough to be enjoyed on the rocks or as a dry martini. It truly is the "utility" gin of our time.

name: Diplomático Reserva Exclusiva Rum 70cl
description: Alc Vol: 40% Size: 70cl A snazzy looking rum all the way from Venezuela, Diplomático Reserva Exclusiva is consistently ranked among the finest rums in the world. Diplomático Reserva Exclusiva is a molasses based rum that is distilled in copper pot stills and matured in whiskey barrels. A refined and elegant blend of exclusive rum reserves masterfully crafted. It boast aromas of orange peel, toffee and licorice, giving this refined rum a signature smooth taste. Marrying a unique body with excellent balance, this multi-award winning rum** is a true reference for fine spirits lovers throughout the world. Perfect to enjoy neat or in sophisticated cocktails. Top stuff for sipping neat or over ice, but also a very good bottle to have on hand when rum cocktails are on the menu. If you're feeling particularly experimental, try using Diplomático Reserva Exclusiva in place of a whiskey in a Manhattan. It works excellently. This fab rum was awarded a Gold Medal at the International Sugar Cane Spirits Tasting Competition in Ybor City.

name: Disaronno Originale Amaretto 500ml
description: Alc Vol: 28% Size: 50cl This traditional digestif is hugely popular—and it’s easy to see why. Disaronno is all about Battenberg cake and deep marzipan flavours. It will satisfy your sweet tooth. Disaronno is the most famous Amaretto liqueur brand in the world, first launched in 1525 Contrary to what most people think, it is not made from almonds—it gets its nuttiness from apricot pits The nose gets an instant whack of sweetness, with strong almond and marzipan aromas Primarily a digestif, but can also be added to modern cocktails Try making a Disaronno Julia cocktail by blitzing together Disaronno (40 ml), white rum (15 ml), liquid cream (25 ml), grenadine (15 ml) and fresh strawberries (4) Serve in a chilled martini glass with strawberry garnish

name: Disaronno Velvet Liqueur 50cl
description: ABV: 17% Size: 500ml Disaronno Velvet is brought to you from the family who discovered the world’s favourite Italian liqueur, Disaronno in 1525. The much-loved Disaronno has made its first foray into cream liqueurs with this Velvet expression! It's the brand's first line extension in over 500 years. It is a distinctly smooth light creamy liqueur that seduces your senses with toasted almonds, apricot, vanilla and a hint of toffee. Velvet is an innovation that will challenge and excite your taste buds. It is the romantic coming together of classic Disaronno mixed with the refreshingly silky smooth cream texture that lingers on the tongue and on your mind long after you put the cap back on.

name: Dolce & Amari Aperitivo 70cl - Non Alcoholic
description: Alc Vol: Non Alcoholic Size: 70cl Premium Infused & Distilled Bitter Orange & Herbal Aperitif, non-alcoholic vibrante Aperitif. Sicilian blood orange meets tropical fruit and is clearly a lively new aperitif highlight. It's clear: the bright pink colour is eye-catching. Its vibrant colouring already gives it away: A drink for warm and cosy summer evenings. And that's exactly what it is, but of course you can serve it however you like. Because its charm also outlasts the warmer days. Then add a few ice cubes and garnish the drink with a slice of lime. Believe us: it's a highlight among summer drinks!

name: Dom Perignon 1998 Vintage Champagne Gift Box 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 12.5% Size: 75cl This Dom Perignon 1998 Comes in a branded gift box. On nose The initial notes of fresh almonds and grapefruit gradually lead into cashew nuts and spices complemented by lightly toasted brioche. On palate The sensation is one of entering a wave: a satiny texture curls and unfurls on the palate, embracing and caressing it. An instant of weightlessness… matter vanishes, the finish vibrates, the taste buds are aroused by a controlled ardor. The persistence is remarkable, with just the slightest undertone of tartness (citrus zest and buds). The harvest The year was marked by two unusual and opposing occurrences: the spectacular burning of the grapes caused by record high temperatures in August followed by exceptional rainfall in the first two weeks of September. The harvest was delayed and the wait was rewarded with the miraculously fine weather that followed. The grapes were healthy and at peak maturity. Technical Information: Vineyards: 100% Grand & Premier Cru Grape Varieties: 51% Chardonnay, 49% Pinot Noir Lees Ageing: 8 years Dosage: 6 g/l Tasting Note: Now some years on from the initial release, these champagnes are starting to show how good they are. On the nose hints of ripe fruits and toastiness lead to a palate full of flavour. Exotic fruits combine with a slight touch of that creamy vanilla richness from its long ageing both on and off the lees. A full and lasting finish pays compliment to a vintage that today is showing better than ever.

name: Dom Perignon 2002 Vintage Champagne 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 12.5% Size: 75cl Dom Pérignon is only available as a vintage champagne and is only produced in exceptional years. Each vintage is a creation, singular and unique, that expresses both the character of the year, and the character of Dom Pérignon. 2002 was one of the all-time great vintages in Champagne and this is a brilliant release from the incredibly consistent Dom Perignon that is perfect for drinking now. A blend of 51% Chardonnay and 49% Pinot Noir sourced solely from Grands and Premiers Crus, it exhibits wonderfully intense aromas of rich stone fruits and lightly toasted almonds, with alluring floral nuances. After at least eight years of lees ageing in the cellars, the wine enters its first phase of interest, Plenitude 1, showing quite youthful fruit and broad texture from the lees contact. The signature Dom Pérignon aromatic is that "hint smoky" character a result of the reductive winemaking where they go to extreme lengths to keep each step of the winemaking process free of oxygen. With every sip of Dom Perignon you always have that purity of fruit character, depending on the vintage you normally see the Pinot Noir fruit dominate, which are those red fruits like raspberry, peach and dark cherry. The Chardonnay adds a supporting layer of chalky texture and delicious fruit in the yellow spectrum like plum, apple and tropical notes of pineapple. Dom Pérignon 2002 expresses all the power of grapes picked at the peak of their maturity, while going far beyond the character of the year, enhancing its natural richness and lending it extra precision and depth. Weather: The 2002 vintage shaped up ideally over the spring, with no significant frost and near-perfect flowering. Then followed a summer marked by long, sunny periods interspersed with regular cloudy and rainy spells. The vines were in good health and the dehydration of the grape berries helped them reach new heights of ripeness. News Article: Dom Pérignon 2002 performed well in our comprehensive Prestige Cuvées from 2002 blind tasting in October 2016 with Jancis Robinson MW. Technical Information: Vineyards: 100% Grand & Premier Cru Grape Varieties: 51% Chardonnay, 49% Pinot Noir Lees Ageing: 7 years Disgorged: July 2009 Dosage: 6 g/l Drink: Now to 2028 Tasting Note: On the nose, the first hints of fresh almond immediately open up into preserved lemon and dried fruits, rounded off by darker smokey and toasted qualities. On the palate, it is creamy, energetic and warm, focusing on the fruit, then gradually taking on more profound bass notes. Dom Pérignon 2002 is a magnetic, still-elusive wine that fully reveals the nature of this legendary House. PLEASE NOTE: This

name: Dom Perignon Champagne Vintage 1999 750ml
description: Alc Vol: 12.5% Size: 75cl This Dom Perignon 1999 does not have a branded gift box. Dom Pérignon is only available as a vintage champagne and is only produced in exceptional years. Each vintage is a creation, singular and unique, that expresses both the character of the year, and the character of Dom Pérignon. After at least eight years of lees ageing in the cellars, the wine enters its first phase of interest, Plenitude 1, showing quite youthful fruit and broad texture from the lees contact. The signature Dom Pérignon aromatic is that "hint smoky" character a result of the reductive winemaking where they go to extreme lengths to keep each step of the winemaking process free of oxygen. With every sip of Dom Perignon you always have that purity of fruit character, depending on the vintage you normally see the Pinot Noir fruit dominate, which are those red fruits like raspberry, peach and dark cherry. The Chardonnay adds a supporting layer of chalky texture and delicious fruit in the yellow spectrum like plum, apple and tropical notes of pineapple. Weather: The 1999 season began with hailstorms in May which destroyed many vines, though as the year went on warmer weather prevailed throughout July and August. The year developed promisingly with high expectations, until heavy rainfall in the second week of September contributed to the loss of many crops. Despite the rain the grapes had maturated well in the long summer and early concerns over dilution of flavour never became an issue. Technical Information: Vineyards: 100% Grand & Premier Cru Grape Varieties: 51% Chardonnay, 49% Pinot Noir Lees Ageing: 8 years Dosage: 6 g/l Drink: Now to 2024 Tasting Note: Now some years on from the initial release, these champagnes are starting to show how good they are. On the nose hints of ripe fruits and toastiness lead to a palate full of flavour. Exotic fruits combine with a slight touch of that creamy vanilla richness from its long ageing both on and off the lees. A full and lasting finish pays compliment to a vintage that today is showing better than ever.

name: Dom Perignon Champagne Vintage 2000 750ml
description: Alc Vol: 12.5% Size: 750ml This Dom Perignon 2000 does not have a branded gift box. Dom Pérignon is only available as a vintage champagne and is only produced in exceptional years. Each vintage is a creation, singular and unique, that expresses both the character of the year, and the character of Dom Pérignon. After at least eight years of lees ageing in the cellars, the wine enters its first phase of interest, Plenitude 1, showing quite youthful fruit and broad texture from the lees contact. The signature Dom Pérignon aromatic is that "hint smoky" character a result of the reductive winemaking where they go to extreme lengths to keep each step of the winemaking process free of oxygen. With every sip of Dom Perignon you always have that purity of fruit character, depending on the vintage you normally see the Pinot Noir fruit dominate, which are those red fruits like raspberry, peach and dark cherry. The Chardonnay adds a supporting layer of chalky texture and delicious fruit in the yellow spectrum like plum, apple and tropical notes of pineapple. Weather: The 2000 vintage was seen by many as a miraculous year. The vines were hit by heavy rain, storms and hail in the winter, leaving a successful harvest on the brink. However, the vintage was saved by the hottest August and September in fifty years, providing the grapes with much needed sunshine and warmth. Technical Information: Vineyards: 100% Grand & Premier Cru Grape Varieties: 51% Chardonnay, 49% Pinot Noir Lees Ageing: 7 years Disgorged: July 2008 Dosage: 6 g/l Drink: Now to 2030 Tasting Note: On the nose, the first hints of fresh almond immediately open up into preserved lemon and dried fruits, rounded off by darker smokey and toasted qualities. On the palate, it is creamy, energetic and warm, focusing on the fruit, then gradually taking on more profound bass notes. Dom Pérignon 2000 is a magnetic, still-elusive wine that fully reveals the nature of this legendary House.

name: Dom Perignon Champagne Vintage 2003 750ml
description: Alc Vol: 12.5% Size: 750ml This 75cl bottle of Dom Pérignon 2003 does not have an individual branded gift box. Dom Pérignon is only available as a vintage champagne and is only produced in exceptional years. Each vintage is a creation, singular and unique, that expresses both the character of the year, and the character of Dom Pérignon. After at least eight years of lees ageing in the cellars, the wine enters its first phase of interest, Plenitude 1, showing quite youthful fruit and broad texture from the lees contact. The signature Dom Pérignon aromatic is that "hint smoky" character a result of the reductive winemaking where they go to extreme lengths to keep each step of the winemaking process free of oxygen. With every sip of Dom Perignon you always have that purity of fruit character, depending on the vintage you normally see the Pinot Noir fruit dominate, which are those red fruits like raspberry, peach and dark cherry. The Chardonnay adds a supporting layer of chalky texture and delicious fruit in the yellow spectrum like plum, apple and tropical notes of pineapple. Weather: The 2003 vintage was one of pre-eminence: a year like no other, defined by extremes. The vineyard was first touched by severe spring frosts that left a lasting mark on the Champagne region before being struck with an unparalleled heatwave. As a result, the crop was perfectly ripe and healthy but left a particularly small harvest: this was the most precocious since 1822. Technical Information: Vineyards: 100% Grand & Premier Cru Grape Varieties: 53% Pinot Noir, 47% Chardonnay Ageing: 8 years on the lees Dosage: 5 g/l Drink: Now to 2030 Tasting Note: The bouquet spirals through a light-filled, floral softness to the minerality that epitomises Dom Pérignon. On the palate, a striking demanding aromatic with bundles of fruits and structure. The finish is long. One of Richard Geoffrey's favourite vintages to create due to the hot weather of the vintage.

name: Dom Pérignon Champagne Vintage 2004 750ml - NO Box
description: Alc Vol: 12.5% Size: 750ml This 2004 vintage of Dom Perignon Does NOT Come with a Gift Box Tasting Notes: Considered as a Contemporary Classic, Dom Perignon Vintage 2004 shows a propensity for growth and expansion, as well as solar qualities with dark and complex underpinnings. On the nose, aromas of almond and powdered cocoa develop gradually into white fruit with hints of dried flowers. Classic toasted notes give a rounded finish and denote a fully realized maturity. On the palate, the wine instantly traces an astoundingly fine line between density and weightlessness. Its precision is extreme, tactile, dark and chiselled. The full taste lingers with the utmost elegance on a sappy spicy note. 2004 Vintage: Dom Perignon sources in the seamless perfect assemblage of selected Pinot Noir and Chardonnay grapes. Playing with contrasts and complementary part, Dom Perignon creates a well-balanced tension of tradition and modernity to interpret the core of its universe. This new Vintage 2004 unveils an elegant and restrained contemporary classicism. "The weather was nearly perfect in 2004," says Richard Geoffroy, Dom Perignon's Chef de Cave, and the year rolled by effortlessly. The 2004 vintage clearly demonstrates that ease, but its true personality was only revealed thanks to the dry heat during the last few weeks. The growing and fruit ripening periods went by smoothly, with no difficulties, a phenomenon that we have rarely, if ever, seen before. The 2004 vintage leaves its mark on the history of Dom Perignon for its ease and harmony. In contrast with the year before, vine growth was regular and progressed without incident, while the bunches were of a significant number and size. The weather remained moderate for some time with the month of August particularly cool the vintage was defined by the dry heat of the final weeks before the harvest. The harvest began on September 24th with the grapes displaying excellent ripeness and health. It is the Chef de Cave's prerogative to decide when a vintage should see the light of day. After no fewer than eight years of aging on the lees, Dom Perignon Vintage 2004 now reveals its first radiant expression, with the promise of more plenitudes to come. Right from the start, Vintage 2004 opens up in an astonishing way, with great ease, extroverted and symbiotic. Gradually, with rare elegance, it glides between density and buoyancy in a seamless crescendo leading into the chiseled complexity of a singular universe. Review: "Another stellar wine, the 2004 Dom Pérignon is just starting to show the first signs of aromatic development, as well as a bit of added weight it did not have as a young wine. The 2004 remains a bright, mid-weight DP built on persistence and length more than overt volume. I have always had a soft spot for the 2004. This tasting does nothing to dampen that enthusiasm. Drink 2019-2039." 97/100 Antonio Galloni, Vinous.com

name: Dom Perignon Champagne Vintage 2006 - Gift Box 750ml
description: Alc Vol: 12.5% Size: 75cl This Dom Perignon 2006 comes in a branded gift box. Dom Pérignon is only available as a vintage champagne and is only produced in exceptional years. Each vintage is a creation, singular and unique, that expresses both the character of the year, and the character of Dom Pérignon. After at least eight years of lees ageing in the cellars, the wine enters its first phase of interest, Plenitude 1, showing quite youthful fruit and broad texture from the lees contact. The signature Dom Pérignon aromatic is that "hint smoky" character a result of the reductive winemaking where they go to extreme lengths to keep each step of the winemaking process free of oxygen. With every sip of Dom Perignon you always have that purity of fruit character, depending on the vintage you normally see the Pinot Noir fruit dominate, which are those red fruits like raspberry, peach and dark cherry. The Chardonnay adds a supporting layer of chalky texture and delicious fruit in the yellow spectrum like plum, apple and tropical notes of pineapple. Weather: With similar weather characteristics as the now-legendary 2002, the 2006 vintage was overall very hot and dry. Vines grew in fits and starts with a heatwave during July succeeded by a cool and humid August. The ultimate factor was the summery September weather, which assisted the grapes in developing much more maturity than usual. As a result, the harvest was the longest on record: four weeks beginning on 11th September. Technical Information: Vineyards: 100% Grand & Premier Cru Grape Varieties: 60% Chardonnay, 40% Pinot Noir Lees Ageing: 8 years Dosage: 5 g/l Drink: Now to 2030 Tasting Note: Initial impressions on the nose are floral and ripe tropical fruits that open to more candied fruit and toasted notes. On the palate an increasing level of creaminess and complexity develop with time in the glass. This is a champagne showing the true hallmarks of a superb vintage which, given time, will position itself as one of the vintages of the decade.

name: Dom Perignon Champagne Vintage 2008 - Gift Box 750ml
description: Alc Vol: Size: 75cl in branded Gift box. When talking about the 2008 vintage it hardly becomes more legendary. The 2008 Vintage is known as one of the best if not the best vintage ever in Champagne. The 2008 Dom Pérignon scores more than 2.5 points on average than the legendary 1988 vintage, The 2008 was the first vintage to be held back and released out of sequence. This was done to ensure that it had achieved the perfect balance. This is a stunningly impressive Champagne with an abundance of white flower, citrus and stone fruit aroma. Pure and sleek, on the palate, the fruit is well-defined, tempered by fresh acidity and enhanced by subtle smokiness. This Champagne, named after the legendary Benedictine monk, is the flagship wine from Moet et Chandon. -Dom Perignon Brut, 2008,

name: Dom Perignon Champagne Vintage 2009 750ml
description: Alc Vol: 12.5% Size: 750ml There is no gift box with this 2009 vintage of Dom Perignon. Dom Pérignon is only available as a vintage champagne and is only produced in exceptional years. Each vintage is a creation, singular and unique, that expresses both the character of the year, and the character of Dom Pérignon. After at least eight years of lees ageing in the cellars, the wine enters its first phase of interest, Plenitude 1, showing quite youthful fruit and broad texture from the lees contact. The signature Dom Pérignon aromatic is that "hint smoky" character a result of the reductive winemaking where they go to extreme lengths to keep each step of the winemaking process free of oxygen. With every sip of Dom Perignon you always have that purity of fruit character, depending on the vintage you normally see the Pinot Noir fruit dominate, which are those red fruits like raspberry, peach and dark cherry. The Chardonnay adds a supporting layer of chalky texture and delicious fruit in the yellow spectrum like plum, apple and tropical notes of pineapple. Weather: The 2009 vintage was a difficult year at first with a mild and rainy spring. The threat of mildew was high and storms in July further raised fears for the health of the grapes. However, a warm August of uninterrupted hot and dry weather was the catalyst for an idyllic harvest. Picking began on the 12th September in perfect conditions. Technical Information: Vineyards: 100% Grand & Premier Cru Grape Varieties: 60% Pinot Noir | 40% Chardonnay Lees Ageing: 7 years Dosage: 4 g/l Alcohol: 12.5% Drink: Now to 2030 Tasting Note: The heat of the year produced grapes of incredible ripeness, reflected in Geoffroy’s decision to use a much lower dosage for Dom Pérignon 2009: just 4 g/l. Already very approachable, it surprisingly doesn’t have the obvious signs of a warm year. A blend from a total of twenty-one villages, Dom Pérignon 2009 is an impressive champagne. It has a silky texture and a hint of liquorice on the palate. Flavours of grapefruit and white peaches lead to a long finish. This Dom Pérignon is tasting well now but will benefit significantly from some time ageing in the bottle. Dom Perignon 2009 does not have a branded gift box

name: Dom Perignon Rose Champagne Vintage 1996 750ml
description: Alc Vol: 12.5% Size: 750ml The 1996 is a dazzling rosé champagne from Dom Pérignon. 1996 was a great year for Dom Pérignon across all the cuvees and it shows here. This is rich and full bodied with a spicy finish, just delightful. Firm, dry and stuffed with raspberry and strawberry flavours. "The 1996 Dom Pérignon Rosé is simply off the charts. What a wine. The 1996 Rose has begun to fill out rather dramatically. Today it is rich, powerful and totally seductive", Antonio Galloni.

name: Domaine d'Hondrat Cuvee Elle & Lui Rose 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 12.5% Size: 75cl Country: France Grape Variety: 60% Grenache Gris, 40% Mourvédre The Domaine d'Hondrat Cuvée Elle and Lui Rosé is served in a unique glass bottle with a glass stopper. "Elle & Lui" translates from French as "she and him", expressing the love between two people, meaning this is a wine full of love and passion. The owner, Romain Vigroux has always kept in mind his great-grandfather's words, "Wine is a matter of Tradition and Family through passion", and this thesis can be felt throughout their fantastic wines. Tasting Notes: The wine is a very pale pink colour with silvery glints. It has subtle aromas of white fruits and tangerine notes on the nose. The wine has an amazing roundness, which is well-balanced by a freshness, supported by citrus zest notes on the finish. Suitable for vegetarians / Vegans

name: Domaine de Courtal Fitou 2011 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 13.5% Size: 75cl A deep, concentrated red color. The first impression on the nose is one of complex aromas of jammy red fruit followed by notes of raspberry and prune from the carefully controlled extraction during vinification and from the exceptional ripeness of the grapes. Its full, fat body and its silky tannins contribute to its superb elegance.On the palate, the powerful flavor is backed by a lovely bouquet of aromas mingling notes of very ripe fruit with touches of leather and under-wood.

name: Domaine Felines Jourdan Picpoul de Pinet 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 13% Size: 75cl Country: France Grape Variety: Picpoul Vintage: 2021 Domaine Félines Jourdan Picpoul de Pinet is a refreshing white wine from the Languedoc region in France, made from 100% Picpoul grapes. This wine displays a pale gold colour with green hues. It presents lively aromas of citrus fruits and white flowers. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with flavours of lemon and green apple, culminating in a clean, refreshing finish.

name: Don Julio 1942 Tequila 70cl - Gift Box
description: ABV - 38% Size - 70cl - Gift Box The legendary distillery of La Primavera is located 6500 feet above sea level in the highlands of Jalisco, Mexico. With rich volcanic soil and an extreme climate, the conditions are perfect for the cultivation of blue agave, bringing the potential for the world's finest tequila. Tasting Notes: Rich caramel and chocolate aromas, with warm oak and vanilla notes on the palate. Roasted agave with a lingering oak and vanilla finish. Serving Suggestions: Serve straight or on the rocks.

name: Don Julio 70 Cristalino Anejo Tequila 70cl - Limited Edition - Boxed
description: Alc Vol: 35% Size: 70cl The world's first anejo cristalino tequila, bringing together the smooth crispness of a blanco with the complexity of an añejo for a totally unique tequila experience. Celebrating 70 years of premium tequila, Don Julio 70 is a superb expression crafted a little differently. Aged in bourbon casks then filtered through charcoal to remove the colour. The result a sweet and citrusy tequila with flavours of honey, chocolate, hints of peppery spice and toasted oak.

name: Don Julio Reposado Tequila 70cl - Gift Box
description: ABV - 38% Size - 70cl - Gift Box Don Julio's Tequila Reposado is one of the most interesting examples of this traditional Mexican distillate, made from the best quality blue agave. Thanks to a production process still carried out with artisanal care and to the refining in noble woods, the Tequila presents a soft and persuasive face, particularly rich and intense from an aromatic point of view. Its velvety taste expresses fresh notes of citrus fruits, ripe fruit and spices. Tequila Reposado is the fruit of the passion of an artisan working in the hills of Atotonilco in the region of Jalisco. Don Julio Gonzàlez was one of the revolutionary innovators in the world of Tequila. He worked all his life to try to produce a softer, more refined distillate and crowned his dream with a series of labels of absolute excellence, which today are among the most loved by enthusiasts. The lands of the Atotonilco area are rich in minerals and provide an ideal habitat for blue agave plants. The plants are harvested by hand and the hearts of the agave are subjected to the process of processing and distillation until a very pure distillate is obtained. Subsequent ageing in wood gives the Tequila its golden colour and refined tertiary aromas. Don Julio's Reposado is a Tequila that comes from a process that is still largely carried out by hand, under the watchful eye of Mastro Tequilero Don Julio who personally chooses the best blue agave plants that are at least 10-12 years old and particularly rich in sugar. At the end of the agave fermentation process and subsequent distillation, the Tequila matures for 8 months in American white oak barrels before being bottled. Tasting Notes: -Colour: It has an intense and bright golden yellow colour. -Aroma: The nose offers persuasive notes of citrus, lime, ripe yellow fruit, soft spices, hints of vanilla and dark chocolate. -Taste: In the mouth is harmonious, with an elegant and delicate taste, which opens on pleasant aromatic facets, with hints of dried fruit, spices and caramel, and then close with a very persistent finish. Serving Suggestions: It is a fine distillate, perfect to be enjoyed neat or to prepare exclusive cocktails with an exotic taste.

name: Don Papa Baroko Spirit Drink 70cl
description: Alc Vol: 38% Size: 70cl This spirit is made with Negros Island sugar cane molasses, distilled in the foothills of Mount Kanlaon before being blended to perfection. With exuberant notes of candied fruits, citrus, and vanilla, Don Papa Baroko is rich and smooth, perfect straight, on the rocks, or in your favourite cocktail. ‘Don Papa’ is a tribute to Papa Isio, the real and charismatic leader from Negros who, in the 1890s, helped drive the Spanish from the island and win independence for Sugarlandia. Don Papa Rum is created on the foothills of the active volcano Mt. Kanlaon in the Philippines, where the finest sugar cane is milled to produce sweet, rich molasses. Don Papa is the embodiment of the Spirit of Sugarlandia.

name: Don Q Anejo Dark Rum 70cl
description: For 6 generations, the Serrallés family has been distilling fine, Puerto Rican rums for over 150 years. Originally from Cataluna, Spain, the Serrallés family migrated to Puerto Rico in the 1830’s where they established a sugar cane plantation and later imported a still in 1865 to produce rum. The Don Q brand was launched in 1932, inspired by the character Don Quixote. We like to think Puerto Rico has the happiest angels in all the world: when we age our Añejo rum, we lose up to 10 percent to evaporation. Compare that to Scotland, which loses only 2 percent to the so-called “Angel’s Share.” Aged in American white oak barrels from 3 to 10 years, Añejo offers a bouquet and mouthfeel that rum experts find… well… intoxicating. ABV 40%

name: Don Ramón Añejo Punta Diamante Tequila 70cl
description: Alc Vol: 38% Size: 70cl A splendid añejo Tequila from Don Ramón, this addition to the Punta Diamante series is produced from 100% agave, and rested for 12 months in American oak barrels before bottling. With satisfying layers of oaken vanilla, cooked peach, and vibrant, peppery agave, it's an enjoyable sipper, with a fancy jewel-shaped stopper!

name: Don Ramon Espadin Mezcal 70cl
description: ABV: 40% Size: 70cl 100% Maguey Espadiin Don Ramón Espadín is a joven mezcal made from 100% Espadin agave grown in Zacatecas, Mexico. Mezcal Espadín comes to life from the privileged land where the legendary Espadín agave grows and is harvested. This gives it a rich and distinguished smoky flavor of earth and wood, as well as a robust and complex character. Similarly, its smoky, herbal, and meaty aroma tells the pride we feel for this land and the life that springs from it. Each presentation is preserved in a spectacular Oaxaca-style bottle with a charred finish and beautiful relief screen prints that enhance its spectacular nature. AGAVE : 100% Young Espadín Agave COLOR : Crystal clear with straw hues AROMA : Smoky, herbal, and meaty NOTES : Smoky and meaty to the palate, herbal with hints of ripened fruits

name: Don Ramón Limited Edition Swarovski Plata Tequila Gift Box 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 40% Size: 70cl Tequila produced in limited quantities in collaboration with the well-known Austrian brand Swarovski®. From field to flute, every step of Tequila Don Ramón Limited Edition is artfully composed by the maestro tequilero. Exceptional standards created by the maestro to cultivate this complex agave elixir, ensures consistency in profile for the discerning palate. Made with 100% blue agave, these limited edition Don Ramon tequilas are presented in an exclusive bottle embellished with Swarovski® crystals. The box is accompanied by a necklace and earrings from the same Austrian house. These tequilas are designed to catch the eye and delight the tastes of the world's most discerning palates .

name: Don Ramón Plata Punta Diamante Tequila 70cl
description: Double distilled Tequila from Don Ramón, made from 100% blue agave, which is harvested from the highlands of Jalisco. It's unaged, with plenty of herbaceous spice and floral notes alongside creamy agave.

name: Donatella Cinelli Colombini Brunello di Montalcino Riserva 2005
description: Alc Vol: 14% Size: 75cl Country: Italy Grape Variety: 100% Sangiovese Vintage: 2005 Riserva Brunello Riserva is one of the finest red wines in the world because of its extraordinary quality and because it is apt for a very long ageing. It can last ages. Brunello does not become Riserva; it is born to be Riserva right from the beginning, right from the moment the grapes are chosen to make it. It is the Montalcino terroir that is an authentic masterpiece. Brunello di Montalcino Riserva by Donatella Cinelli Colombini is produced from Sangiovese grapes from Casato Prime Donne, a property of about 40 hectares with 17 of vineyard, situates on the northern slope of Montalcino. This is the cooler area of the Brunello territory where the ripening are slower and more regular thanks also to the soils that are not very fertile and that give wines characterized by high sensorial intensity and floral and fruit notes. Brunello is produced only in great vintages when the climate creates small and lose clusters with small berries that are perfectly ripe. Donatella Cinelli Colombini dreams of going back to the original style of Brunello, that that she discovered as a child in the wines made by her Grandfather Giovanni, one of the pioneers in the Montalcino denomination. Elegant wines, that are vertical, fresh and with a strong territorial imprint as well as the ability to be cellared for a long time. The project “original style” began in 2011 through a return to manual labour and old traditions, like the use of concrete tanks for fermentation. Donatella is very connected to traditions and does not forget that Casato Prime Donne already belonged to her ancestors more than 400 years ago. Bottles produced: from 5.000 to 10.000 depending on the vintage. Riserva is produced only in the best vintages Awards: Each year it is produced the Brunello Riserva by Donatella Cinelli Colombini gets high ratings from the most important specialized international press. Among these are: Wine Enthusiast put it in 14th position of their TOP 100 in 2005. COLOUR Ruby red that in tie becomes garnet. Brilliant, sufficiently intense, in the glass the movement is slow thanks to the richness in noble components. AROMA Fine, complex, deep, with hints of ripe red berries and fruits, and of spices TASTE Very elegant, the tannic and acid structure seems perfectly balanced by the fruit and alcohol, it is fresh and vertical and its tannins are silky. There is a long persistence in the mouth. Matching food: Important meat dishes and aged cheeses. SERVING TEMPERATURE Alcohol content: 14,5-15% depending on the vintage. Serving temperature: 19-20 °C 66-68°F m in transparent crystal balloon shaped glasses. It is advisable to open a couple of hours before serving or to use a decanter.

name: Donovan's Black Forest Gateau Country Cream 70cl
description: Size: 70cl Alc Vol:12% Made with a sumptuous blend of the finest cream and rich chocolate, and cherry flavours. This velvety country cream melts on the tongue. Enjoy it straight from the bottle over ice or use it to improve your favourite cocktails and desserts. Made with real double cream, cocoa extract, and all natural flavours.

name: Donovan's Hot Cross Bun Country Cream 70cl - NEW
description: Alc Vol: 12% Size: 70cl Limited Stocks Available......When it's gone it's gone.... Donovan's Hot Cross Bun Country Cream Brace yourselves people, just launched a brand-new liqueur for Easter 2026 and it’s inspired by one of our all-time favourite easter treats... Hot Cross Buns! If you love Hot Cross Buns and you love a glass of Baileys, this could be your dream Easter tipple. This limited-edition bottle is a cream liqueur inspired by the iconic sweet spiced, bread buns Studded with raisins or currants and flavored with spices like cinnamon and nutmeg. While historically reserved for Good Friday, they are now widely available throughout the Easter. Give it a good shake before pouring.

name: Donovan's Pumpkin Spice Latte Country Cream 70cl
description: Size: 70cl Alc Vol: 12% Made with a sumptuous blend of the finest cream and pumpkin, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, and coffee flavours. This velvety cream liqueur melts on the tongue. Made with real double cream & all natural flavours.

name: Donovan's Speculoos Caramelised Biscuit Country Cream 70cl
description: Size: 70cl Alc Vol: 12% Made with a sumptuous blend of the finest cream and caramel, cinnamon, and biscuity flavours. This velvety country cream melts on the tongue. Enjoy it straight from the bottle over ice or use it to improve your favourite cocktails and desserts. Made with real double cream & all natural flavours.

name: Donovan's Strawberries & Cream Country Cream Liqueur 70CL
description: Made with a sumptuous blend of the finest cream and a splash of strawberry. This velvety Cream Liqueur melts on the tongue, leaving behind a delicious strawberry flavour. Enjoy it straight from the bottle over ice or use it to create your favourite cocktails or desserts. Made with real cream & all natural flavours.

name: Dooleys Toffee Liqueur 70cl
description: Experience the luxurious Taste of World's most awarded cream Liqueur. Delicious Dooley's Original Toffee Vodka liqueur is created using a blend of Premium Vodka with the finest Dutch Cream and Excellent Belgian Toffee. A rich and creamy liqueur for a uniquely indulgent drinking experience. Dooley's Original Toffee Vodka liqueur has the gentle aroma of fresh sweet cream, rich toffee and roasted nuts providing mildly sweet, soft flavours with great intensity and depth. Half the fat of traditional cream liqueurs due to its vodka base, Dooley's enjoys unrivalled mixability making it the perfect drink on its own over ice or as the base of many delicious, creamy and indulgent cocktails Distilled in Northern Germany by Behn Originals, the family owned company has been producing premium spirits since 1892 and have created Dooley's Original Toffee Vodka with 50% less fat than most other cream liqueurs. Dooley’s all natural ingredients combined in a secret blending process give this liqueur it’s incredibly smooth taste, texture and ability to be mixed widely, makes this the perfect drink alone, over ice and as a luxurious base for cocktails.

name: Doom Bar Cornish Ale Pint (Blue Logo) Beer Glass
description: Take it easy and refresh yourself with a nice Cold drink. Enjoy your favourite 'beverage' in this Doom Bar Pint Glass. One of Britain's most popular beers and brewed in Cornwall with only finest quality ingredients, these Doombar Glasses are the obvious choice for showcasing your favourite Doombar beer. With a grey Doombar logo and factoids wrapped around the glass, these branded pint glasses add a unique touch to your drinks service. These glasses are made with fully toughened glass, which is stronger therefore increasing longevity and withstands greater impact. Lined and CE marked at a pint, these robust glasses are perfect for serving up beer in licensed establishments. (Box 162)

name: Doom Bar Keg 50lt / 11 Gall / 88 Pints
description: ABV: 4.3% SIZE : 50lt / 11 Gall / 88 Pints FITTING : Grundy G Type Type : Amber Ale Doom Bar is a perfectly balanced beer combining subtle yet complex flavours. This creates an amber ale which is both satisfying and deliciously moreish. ALSO AVAILABLE FOR COLLECTION FROM: L EICE STER (LE19)

name: Doorly's 3yo White Barbados Rum 70cl
description: AVB 40% Size: 70cl From one of the oldest and most prestigious producers of Rum on the island of Barbados comes Doorly's 3 year old Rum. Unusually for a white Rum, it is aged for a minimum of 3 years in small casks before a 3 step charcoal filtration process to give the final remarkably clear and unique spirit. Mineral with tropical fruit and pineapple. Strong notes of coconut, probably from the American oak casks this was aged in, with faint toffee/fudge note and white peppery spice. Lingering white pepper and coconut on the finish.

name: Doritos Crisps Tangy Cheese Tortilla Chips PM£1.25 18x70g
description: - Outrageously delicious Doritos tortilla chips with a bold flavour and full-on crunch - Seriously cheesy flavoured snacks for sharing with friends - Split a bag with your mates while watching your favourite movie - Why not also try Doritos multipacks for grab-and-go snacks throughout the week? - Suitable for vegetarians Suitable for vegetarians

name: Doritos Extra Flamin' Hot Sharing Bag Crisps PM £1.35 15x70g
description: -Doritos EXTRA Flamin’ Hot Sharing Tortilla Chips 70g - Price Marked Pack -The bold crunch of Doritos is now EXTRA Flamin’ Hot! -Try these and others in the EXTRA Flamin’ Hot crisps range: Walkers Max and Wotsits Crunchy -Suitable for vegetarians. name: Dos Equis - 5L Draught Beer Keg
description: Volume - 5 Litres ABV - 5.0% Bring a taste of Mexico to your next get-together with Dos Equis. This smooth and refreshing lager delivers crisp malt flavours, light bitterness, and an easy-drinking finish that’s made for sharing. Whether you're hosting a BBQ, watching the match, or relaxing with friends, this draught beer keg makes pouring pub-quality beer at home simple. Brewed with balance and drinkability in mind, Dos Equis Lager Especial combines golden malt character with a clean, refreshing finish. At 4.2% ABV, it’s a versatile lager that pairs perfectly with tacos, grilled food, and sunny afternoons. This 5L beer keg can be used with a Beer Tender Machine or poured directly from the keg for easy home draught moments. For the best experience, chill your keg for at least 10 hours before serving at around 4°C. Pour into a tall lager glass to enjoy the crisp aroma and smooth carbonation.

name: Double Diamond Beer 4x440ml - NEW
description: ABV: 3.8% Size: 4 x 440ml Cans Double Diamond is back.... AN iconic beer from the 90s has made a triumphant return and is now available from 365drinks.co.uk Originally brewed by Samuel Allsopp & Sons in 1876, Double Diamond became one of Britain’s biggest-selling drinks throughout the 1950s, ’60s and ’70s, before being discontinued in 2003. The new iteration of Double Diamond takes inspiration from the original 1922 IPA but is made using an all-new recipe "This sessionable Pale Ale is the next iteration of Double Diamond and we hope will find a place in the affections of new drinkers, as well as those that haven’t tasted it in 30 years". Brewed as a Pale Ale, Drinks Like a Lager Tastes Bloody Wonderful! Gluten Free Vegan Friendly

name: Down Under Chardonnay 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 13% Size: 75cl Country: Australia Grape Variety: Chardonnay A great Chardonnay boating zesty tropical aromas and the enticing flavours of white peach & pear.

name: Downton Abbey Premium Gin 70cl
description: ABV - 43% Size - 70cl Botanicals: Juniper, Fresh Lime, Fresh Ginger Root, Rosewater, Harrogate Spring Water and Coriander Seed Downton Abbey Premium Gin is the ‘Official’ Downton Abbey Gin and is expertly concocted using only the finest fresh botanicals and Harrogate Spring Water from North Yorkshire right in the heart of ‘Downton Abbey Country’. Fresh Limes and fresh Ginger Root from the Victorian Hothouse together with English Rose Water make this Gin a drink of true character and distinction – perfect over a game of gin rummy. Chin-chin!

name: Dows Quinta Do Bomfim Vintage 2008 Port 75cl in Wooden Case
description: Alc Vol: 20% Size: 75cl Quinta do Bomfim Vintage Port 2008 is an extraordinary Vintage Port wine that presents an elegant and refined structure, and that highlights in the mouth, a sublime overlay of layers of perfectly defined aromas. It highlights sensory layers of ripe black fruits with hints of vanilla and spices along with spicy and juicy tannins.

name: Dr Squid Gin 70cl
description: ABV: 40% Vol Size: 70cl Dr Squid Gin has been a labour of love dating back to the spring of 2018. "18 months, 16 fights, 14 failed batches, 12 design ideas, 10 shipping fails, 8 missed launch dates, 6 packaging blunders, 4 days in to Lockdown, 2 serious explosions, 1 Dr Squid!!!......Its finally arrived "On many occasions we almost conceded that it would not be possible, neither the copper flask nor the balancing of the recipe, but it was an idea that we so steadfastly believed in that we could not let it go". Dr Squid Gin has been lovingly made by Pocketful of Stones Distillery down at Long Rock, Penzance. This utterly gorgeous unique gin is made with real squid ink from a secret recipe and is served from a handmade copper flask (yes, you read that correctly) engraved with the scenery, wildlife, folklore and history that surrounds them down there. Dr Squid Gin has a savoury expression, with a coastal, Mediterranean freshness. style gin, from coriander, sea buck thorn and squid ink. It has a smooth full mouth feel from vanilla and a slight spice, pepper. Dr Squid Gin opens up when tonic is added release floral and citrus notes of meadow sweet and lemon. Best of all though, wait for it…Dr Squid Gin pours black however when mixed with tonic it turns a vivid pink. Just unbelievably gorgeous to behold and to sip! Wow! Squid Ink is such a powerful ingredient that only a minimal amount is needed both for the distilling and colour or infusion of the gin. Vanilla was added to the base of the recipe to give a well rounded mouthfeel and perceived sweetness , coupled with sea buckthorn for a tart, fresh fruit finish. It also makes for a great Gin Mule when poured over crushed ice and ginger beer.

name: Dr Zenzen Dornfelder Red Wine 0% Alcohol Free 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 0.0% Size 75cl Wine of Germany, Style : Alcohol Free, This Dornfelder red wine is a delightful shiny cherry with subtle aromas of pomegranate & red currant. It has ripe cassis and berry flavours and has had the alcohol removed carefully. Beautiful aromatic Dornfelder - fully alcohol-free Made using a gentle de-alcoholisation process, DR. ZENZEN Dornfelder Alcohol-Free red wine is a stunning feat. Created with ripe cassis and deep, red berry flavours, it has everything you crave in red wine but it’s totally alcohol-free. Created to be paired perfectly with red meat and dark chocolate, it’s full-bodied yet balanced for an all-encompassing mouthfeel, surprising non-believers that non-alcoholic red wine could possibly taste so good. Finally, a red wine that won’t leave you under the table.

name: Dr Zenzen Pinot Grigio Alcohol Free 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 0.0% Size 75cl Wine of Germany, Light-hearted, non-alcoholic wine enjoyment at any time of day. The typical taste of Pinot Grigio in a non-alcoholic version. A German Pinot Grigio that can inspire in this Version.

name: Dr Zenzen Riesling 0% Alcohol Free 75cl
description: Alc Vol: 0.0% Size 75cl Wine of Germany, Style : Alcohol Free, Dry ZENZEN Riesling Alcohol-Free white wine surprises anyone who tastes it with its ability to maintain true Riesling flavour without the boozy aftermath. With hints of lemon, juicy peaches, and a touch of vanilla, it's everything you could want in a premium alcohol-free white wine. This classic non-alcoholic Riesling harmoniously dry, with an ideal balance of sweetness and acidity. Made from the well-known Riesling variety of grapes in the Rheinhessen region of Germany.

name: Dragon Stout 284ml Bottle
description: 7.5% ABV Size: 284ml Bottles Brewed and bottle by Red Stripe, Dragon Stout is often Known as Red Stripe's darker stronger brother, it is Jamaica’s best kept secret, Exclusively produced in Jamaica, it was first brewed in 1920, Dragon Stout became know as the working man’s drink and established is itself as a source of strength and vitality. Still a favorite of most Jamaicans, Dragon Stout holds its own in the market as the popular stout

name: Drambuie Honey Whisky Liqueur - Prince Charles Edward's Liqueur -1 Litre - COLLECTERS ITEM
description: Alc Vol: 40% Size: 1lt An old litre bottling of Drambuie. The whisky, honey, herbs and spice liqueur has been made since the late 19th century. We estimate this bottle dates from the 1980s. In 1745 Bonnie Prince Charlie landed on the West Coast of Scotland. Driven by fiery ambition, he was intent on reclaiming the throne of his ancestors, rallying many to his cause. Despite early victories, they were decisively defeated at the Battle of Culloden and Charlie escaped to the Isle of Skye under the protection of the MacKinnon clan. By way of thanks he gifted them the only possession he had left, the recipe to his personal elixir. They called it Drambuie, after the Gaelic'An Dram Buidheach' which means The drink that satisfies. Aged Scotch with soft spices, fragrant herbs and heavenly real Scottish heather honey

name: Drambuie Honey Whisky Liqueur 50cl
description: Alc Vol: 40% Size: 50CL Drambuie is a Scottish honeyed whisky liqueur, which mellows aged Scotch with soft spices, fragrant herbs and heavenly real Scottish heather honey. This legendary liqueur can be enjoyed in any manner of ways, rich enough to be savoured on its own, neat or on the rocks and its enchanting aromas of butterscotch and shortbread work wonderfully with coffee or paired with a delectable dessert. This rich, honeyed liqueur with layers of luscious flavour can also be lengthened into a refreshingly long drink and offers plenty of options for imaginative hosts looking to delight their guests and extend the evening. Delicious as a post-dinner treat, Drambuie's infusion of honey means it's velvety smooth when sipped neat. Over ice, its fragrant blend of herbs and spices, make it a fuss free ready-made ‘Old Fashioned' style cocktail. Drambuie's rich, enticing honeyed heather aromas combine fantastically well with cream, and so Drambuie is delicious when used in indulgent boozy desserts or liqueur coffees. It is also a perfect pairing to coffee, chocolate or cheese which is why Drambuie is frequently served as that “little extra something” after the main meal. In 1745 Bonnie Prince Charlie landed on the West Coast of Scotland. Driven by fiery ambition, he was intent on reclaiming the throne of his ancestors, rallying many to his cause. Despite early victories, they were decisively defeated at the Battle of Culloden and Charlie escaped to the Isle of Skye under the protection of the MacKinnon clan. By way of thanks he gifted them the only possession he had left, the recipe to his personal elixir. They called it Drambuie, after the Gaelic'An Dram Buidheach' which means The drink that satisfies. Aged Scotch with soft spices, fragrant herbs and heavenly real Scottish heather honey

name: Drambuie Honey Whisky Liqueur 70cl
description: Alc Vol: 40% Size: 70cl Drambuie is one of the best-known whisky liqueurs worldwide. Drambuie's secret recipe includes a blend of aged Scotch whisky, Scottish heather honey, and floral herbs & aromatic spices. Often regarded as either a liqueur or a whisky, it can be an ideal spirit for those wanting to explore the taste of whisky, without the harshness often associated with the whisky taste. The golden honeyed liquid is perfect enjoyed on its own, with or without ice and has a delicious lingering warmth. Drambuie traditionally has been served after dinner as a digestif, and was drunk to aid the digestion of food. Drambuie means it complements food well and is ideal to round off the evening or to offer to guests at casual get-togethers as “a little something” extra, during, after, or instead of dessert, as Drambuie pairs well with coffee, cheese and chocolates. Drambuie also makes a great gift for a cherished one. Drambuie is a very versatile spirit that is an ideal choice for when your entertaining guests as it can be used simply to create a range of cocktails and desserts that are easy and impressive. In fact the lingering honeyed warmth of Drambuie is very similar in taste to a trending cocktail, the Old Fashioned; the classic recipe combines whisky, sugar and aromatic bitters, stirred vigorously with ice. Drambuie already combines all of these ingredients but the bitters are replaced with a blend of floral herbs and aromatic spices … plus it's much simpler to prepare, simply add a garnish such as a slice of orange.
